entries.name: Next.js
entries.role: React framework for full-stack web applications
entries.details: The project uses Next.js 16.1.6 as the primary framework with App Router architecture. There are 129 files in src/app/**/* including 45+ route files and API endpoints. The configuration in next.config.ts shows custom rewrites for resume PDF generation, remote image patterns for Unsplash, and integration with Vanilla Extract CSS via plugin.
facts: 129 files in src/app/**/* directory structure
45+ route files and API endpoints
Custom rewrite from /resume/:slug.pdf to /api/resume-pdf/:slug

entries.name: React
entries.role: UI library for building component-based interfaces
entries.details: React 19.1.1 is used throughout the application with 71 component files in src/components/**/*. The project uses modern React features including hooks and server components. Components are organized into UI components, admin components, and specialized components for job applications and interview prep.
facts: 71 component files in src/components/**/*
Uses React 19.1.1 with React DOM 19.1.1
Component architecture with UI, admin, and domain-specific components

entries.name: Radix UI Themes
entries.role: Design system and component theming
entries.details: Radix UI Themes 3.3.0 provides the design system foundation with Radix UI Icons 1.3.2 for iconography. The theming system is used for consistent styling across the application's UI components. Components like Badge, Button, Card, and SearchInput in src/components/ui/ leverage Radix primitives.
facts: Radix UI Themes 3.3.0 with Radix UI Icons 1.3.2
UI components in src/components/ui/ directory
Design system for consistent theming

entries.name: Clerk
entries.role: Authentication and user management
entries.details: Clerk 6.37.4 handles authentication with dedicated sign-in and sign-up pages at /sign-in and /sign-up routes. The auth-header.tsx component provides authentication UI elements. Clerk integrates with Next.js App Router for protected routes and user session management throughout the application.
facts: Clerk 6.37.4 for authentication
Dedicated /sign-in and /sign-up routes
Auth header component for UI integration

entries.name: Vanilla Extract
entries.role: Zero-runtime CSS-in-TypeScript styling
entries.details: Vanilla Extract 1.18.0 is configured via Next.js plugin for zero-runtime CSS-in-TypeScript. The project uses CSS modules (page.module.css) alongside Vanilla Extract for styling. Components like jobs-list.css.ts and source-filter.css.ts demonstrate Type-safe CSS with build-time extraction.
facts: Vanilla Extract 1.18.0 with Next.js plugin integration
CSS modules alongside Type-safe CSS
Build-time CSS extraction for zero runtime

entries.name: Apollo Server
entries.role: GraphQL server for API layer
entries.details: Apollo Server 5.3.0 is used as the GraphQL server with Node.js runtime for better I/O performance. It's integrated with Next.js via @as-integrations/next and configured with rate limiting (100 requests/minute) and Clerk authentication. The server uses a modular schema system with 34 GraphQL files across schema/ and src/graphql/ directories.
facts: 34 GraphQL schema files found across schema/ and src/graphql/ directories
Rate limiting configured at 100 requests per minute per user/IP
Uses Node.js runtime for better I/O performance with D1 database

source_locations.note: Node.js runtime configuration for better performance

entries.name: Next.js API Routes
entries.role: RESTful API endpoints and GraphQL integration
entries.details: 41 API route files organized under src/app/api/ covering GraphQL, admin endpoints, email generation, resume PDF rendering, and webhooks. Vercel configuration sets 60-second max duration for all API routes. The GraphQL route at /api/graphql serves as the primary API endpoint with authentication and rate limiting.
facts: 41 API route files found in src/app/api/ directory
Vercel timeout configured at 60 seconds max duration for all API routes
GraphQL API route includes Clerk authentication and rate limiting

source_locations.note: Vercel functions configuration with 60-second max duration
GraphQL GET route handler with rate limiting
GraphQL POST route handler with rate limiting

entries.name: GraphQL Tools
entries.role: GraphQL schema generation and tooling
entries.details: @graphql-tools/schema 10.0.31 is used to create executable schemas from type definitions. The project uses GraphQL Code Generator for type safety with TypeScript resolvers. Schema is split across multiple domains including companies, jobs, applications, prompts, and study topics.
facts: GraphQL schema organized across 12 domain directories in schema/
Uses @graphql-tools/load-files and @graphql-tools/schema for schema assembly
GraphQL Code Generator configured for TypeScript type generation

source_locations.note: Executable schema creation with typeDefs and resolvers

entries.name: Clerk Authentication
entries.role: User authentication and session management
entries.details: Clerk 6.37.4 provides authentication for GraphQL API routes. User ID and email are fetched from Clerk sessions and passed to GraphQL context. Development mode includes an ADMIN_EMAIL bypass for local testing without authentication.
facts: User authentication integrated into GraphQL context setup
Development mode includes ADMIN_EMAIL bypass for testing
User email fetched from Clerk for admin checks and prompt access

source_locations.note: Clerk auth() call to get userId
Fetching user email from Clerk client

entries.name: Drizzle ORM
entries.role: Database access layer for API
entries.details: Drizzle ORM 0.45.1 provides database access through Cloudflare D1. Database instance is passed to GraphQL context and used by resolvers. DataLoaders are created for efficient batch loading of related data.
facts: Database instance passed to GraphQL context for all resolvers
DataLoaders created for efficient batch loading
Uses Cloudflare D1 HTTP client for database access

source_locations.note: Database client creation with D1 HTTP client

entries.name: Drizzle ORM
entries.role: Type-safe SQLite ORM for schema definition and query building
entries.details: Used as the primary ORM for defining database schemas and types. The schema is defined in src/db/schema.ts with 15+ table definitions including companies, jobs, user_settings, job_skill_tags, and company_facts. Drizzle Kit (v0.31.8) is used for migrations and database studio. The project uses SQLite core with Cloudflare D1 bindings.
facts: 15+ table definitions in schema.ts
31 migration SQL files in migrations/ directory
Uses drizzle-kit for migrations and studio

entries.name: Cloudflare D1
entries.role: Serverless SQLite database for Cloudflare Workers
entries.details: Used as the primary database backend with D1 bindings. The project has a dedicated D1 Gateway Worker (workers/d1-gateway.ts) that provides a secure HTTP API for database access from Vercel. Database ID is 632b9c57-8262-40bd-86c2-bc08beab713b with binding name 'DB'. Uses wrangler for migrations and deployment.
facts: Database ID: 632b9c57-8262-40bd-86c2-bc08beab713b
D1 Gateway Worker provides 8+ REST endpoints
Uses D1 batch queries for performance

entries.name: Cloudflare Workers
entries.role: Edge runtime for D1 Gateway and database access
entries.details: Hosts the D1 Gateway Worker that provides secure HTTP API access to the D1 database. The worker implements authentication, CORS, and optimized query patterns. Uses wrangler v4.61.1 for deployment with compatibility date 2026-02-01. Worker includes observability with logs enabled.
facts: Wrangler version 4.61.1
Compatibility date 2026-02-01
Observability logs enabled

entries.name: DeepSeek via @ai-sdk/deepseek
entries.role: Primary LLM provider for job classification, SQL generation, email generation, and AI agents
entries.details: Used throughout the project via @ai-sdk/deepseek package. Configured for remote EU job classification in the process-jobs worker (runs hourly via cron), SQL generation agents, and email generation. The system uses multiple DeepSeek models including deepseek-chat, deepseek-reasoner, and deepseek-r1-distill-qwen-32b via Cloudflare Workers AI. Base URL configured as https://api.deepseek.com with beta features available at https://api.deepseek.com/beta.
facts: 6 agents in src/agents/ directory using DeepSeek
Hourly cron schedule (0 * * * *) in workers/process-jobs/wrangler.jsonc
Supports models: deepseek-chat, deepseek-reasoner, deepseek-r1-distill-qwen-32b

entries.name: Anthropic Claude via @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk
entries.role: Agentic AI for job seeking, architect sub-agents, and study topic generation
entries.details: Integrated through @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk package with project settings in .claude/ directory and CLAUDE.md. Used for job seeking agents, architect sub-agents, and study topic generation with models like claude-haiku-4-5-20251001. Includes MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers for PostgreSQL, filesystem, and GitHub integration.
facts: 16 files in src/anthropic/ directory for Claude integration
Supports models: claude-opus-4-6, claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929, claude-haiku-3-5-20241022
Uses .claude/ directory for project settings and skills

entries.name: Vercel AI SDK
entries.role: Unified interface for AI models across the application
entries.details: Provides a unified interface for AI models across the application via the 'ai' package. Used alongside @ai-sdk/deepseek and @ai-sdk/anthropic packages. Enables consistent model calling patterns for SQL generation, study topics, and resume analysis across different LLM providers.
facts: Combined with @ai-sdk/deepseek and @ai-sdk/anthropic packages
Used in 6 agent files for structured output generation
Integrates with Langfuse for AI observability

source_locations.note: Uses Vercel AI SDK with DeepSeek
Classify job using Vercel AI SDK

entries.name: OpenRouter
entries.role: Fallback and comparison gateway for testing multiple DeepSeek models
entries.details: Acts as a fallback and comparison gateway when testing multiple models against the same prompt. Configured with baseURL: https://openrouter.ai/api/v1. Provides access to multiple DeepSeek models including deepseek-chat, deepseek-r1, deepseek-r1-distill-qwen-32b, and deepseek-coder through a unified interface.
facts: 5 DeepSeek models configured in src/openrouter/config.ts
Used for model comparison and fallback scenarios
Base URL: https://openrouter.ai/api/v1

entries.name: Google ADK
entries.role: Google AI integration for Gemini models and AI development
entries.details: Google AI Development Kit for integrating Gemini models and AI capabilities. Used alongside @google/genai package for additional Google AI services. Part of the AI stack for multi-provider AI capabilities.
facts: @google/adk version 0.3.0
@google/genai version 1.41.0
@google/adk-devtools version 0.3.0 for development

entries.name: Langfuse
entries.role: Central LLM observability layer for tracing, prompt management, and scoring
entries.details: Langfuse is configured with LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY, LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY, and LANGFUSE_BASE_URL environment variables pointing to https://cloud.langfuse.com. It's used for runtime prompt management (fetching prompts like 'job-classifier'), tracing all LLM calls, and scoring accuracy via evaluation scripts. Prompts are referenced using '@@@langfusePrompt:name=PromptName|label=production@@@' format and fetched dynamically from Langfuse cloud.
facts: Configured with 3 required environment variables in src/config/env.ts
Prompt references use format '@@@langfusePrompt:name=PromptName|label=production@@@'
Dedicated eval script at scripts/eval-remote-eu-langfuse.ts

source_locations.note: Fetching Langfuse prompts in LLM calls

entries.name: @langfuse/otel
entries.role: OpenTelemetry integration for distributed tracing
entries.details: Provides OpenTelemetry integration that Langfuse builds upon for distributed tracing infrastructure. Enables detailed trace collection and analysis of LLM calls and application workflows through the underlying OpenTelemetry SDK.
facts: @langfuse/otel package version 4.6.1 provides OpenTelemetry integration
Used alongside @opentelemetry/sdk-trace-node version 2.5.1

entries.name: @langfuse/tracing
entries.role: Tracing SDK for Langfuse observability
entries.details: Provides the tracing SDK that integrates with Langfuse for capturing LLM calls, spans, and traces. Works with the OpenTelemetry backend to provide comprehensive observability across the application.
facts: @langfuse/tracing package version 4.6.1
Part of Langfuse's comprehensive observability stack

entries.name: LangSmith
entries.role: Alternative evaluation platform and LLM observability
entries.details: LangSmith is installed as a dependency but appears to be used primarily as a fallback or alternative evaluation platform. The project has evaluation workflows that can run against both Langfuse and LangSmith, with dedicated scorer functions for tracking LLM performance on classification tasks.
facts: langsmith dependency version 0.5.2
Integrated into remote EU evaluation workflows as alternative platform

entries.name: Cloudflare Workers
entries.role: Serverless runtime for background jobs, crawlers, and AI processing
entries.details: Multiple workers handle different tasks: ashby-crawler (Rust/WASM) crawls job boards daily at 02:00 UTC, eu-classifier (Python) runs every 6 hours for role classification, and job-reporter-llm processes job reports via queue. All workers share the same D1 database (nomadically-work-db, ID: 632b9c57-8262-40bd-86c2-bc08beab713b) with remote bindings for local development.
facts: 7 distinct workers found: ashby-crawler, cleanup-jobs, eu-classifier, job-matcher, job-reporter-llm, process-jobs, resume-rag, sdd-agent
3 workers use cron triggers: ashby-crawler (0 2 * * *), eu-classifier (0 */6 * * *), others disabled due to 5-cron free plan limit
All workers bind to same D1 database: nomadically-work-db (632b9c57-8262-40bd-86c2-bc08beab713b)

source_locations.note: Cron disabled due to free plan limit

entries.name: Rust/WASM (ashby-crawler)
entries.role: WebAssembly crawler for job board APIs (Ashby, Greenhouse, Lever, Workable)
entries.details: Rust-based worker compiled to WebAssembly that crawls Common Crawl indexes for job boards. Contains 14 source files in src/ including modules for different ATS providers (ashby.rs, greenhouse.rs, lever.rs, workable.rs). Uses worker-build for compilation and runs on Cloudflare Workers with Rust runtime.
facts: 14 Rust source files in workers/ashby-crawler/src/
4 database migrations (0001_init, 0002_enrichment, 0003_agent_analysis, 0007_greenhouse_boards, 0009_lever_boards, 0011_workable_boards)
Build command: cargo install -q worker-build && worker-build --release

entries.name: Python Workers
entries.role: Python-based workers for AI classification, job processing, and cleanup tasks
entries.details: Python workers use compatibility_flags: ["python_workers"] and handle tasks like EU job classification, job matching, resume RAG, and cleanup. eu-classifier uses LangChain with Cloudflare AI bindings, job-reporter-llm uses DeepSeek API via AI Gateway, and cleanup-jobs handles database maintenance.
facts: 5 Python workers: cleanup-jobs, eu-classifier, job-matcher, process-jobs, resume-rag
All use compatibility_flags: ["python_workers"]
eu-classifier has 8 Python source files in src/

entries.name: Cloudflare Queues
entries.role: Message queue system for async job processing between workers
entries.details: Used for decoupled processing: job-reporter-llm consumes from job-report-queue (max_batch_size: 10), cleanup-jobs has cleanup-jobs-queue (max_batch_size: 1), and both have dead letter queues for failed messages. Queue bindings allow producers to enqueue jobs and consumers to process them asynchronously.
facts: job-reporter-llm queue: max_batch_size=10, max_retries=3, dead_letter_queue=job-report-dlq
cleanup-jobs queue: max_batch_size=1, max_retries=3, dead_letter_queue=cleanup-jobs-dlq
eu-classifier queues disabled due to plan limitations

source_locations.note: Queues disabled due to plan limits

entries.name: Cloudflare D1
entries.role: SQLite-based database for all worker data persistence
entries.details: Shared D1 database (nomadically-work-db) used by all workers with remote=true binding for local development. Database ID: 632b9c57-8262-40bd-86c2-bc08beab713b. Contains tables for jobs, classifications, crawls, and agent sessions managed through migrations in ashby-crawler.
facts: Single database ID: 632b9c57-8262-40bd-86c2-bc08beab713b
All workers use remote=true binding for local dev
6 database migrations in ashby-crawler/migrations/

source_locations.note: D1 database ID and remote binding

entries.name: Trigger.dev
entries.role: Background job processing and scheduling system for job data enhancement
entries.details: Trigger.dev is used for scheduled and on-demand background jobs to enhance job postings from ATS sources like Greenhouse and Ashby. The system has 4 task definitions in src/trigger/ directory: enhanceJobTask, enhanceJobsScheduled (cron), enhanceJobsOnDemand, and processResumeTask. Configuration in trigger.config.ts sets project ID 'proj_gmqcwyqsqcnkjnlqcmxf' with 6-hour job scanning schedule and 5 concurrent task limit.
facts: Found 4 task definitions across 5 files in src/trigger/ directory
Scheduled task runs every 6 hours with cron '0 */6 * * *' to enhance unprocessed jobs
Maximum task duration configured as 3600 seconds (1 hour) with 3 retry attempts default
Project ID: proj_gmqcwyqsqcnkjnlqcmxf with Node.js runtime

source_locations.note: Main enhanceJobTask definition with concurrency limit 5

entries.name: Vitest
entries.role: Testing framework for unit and integration tests
entries.details: Vitest is listed as a dev dependency in package.json at line 115. It's used for testing across the codebase, though no specific test files were found in the evals directory. The project has 6 TypeScript evaluation files in src/evals/ focused on remote EU job classification and AI evaluation tasks.
facts: Vitest version 4.0.18 is installed as dev dependency
6 TypeScript evaluation files exist in src/evals/ directory

entries.name: Promptfoo
entries.role: LLM evaluation and testing framework
entries.details: Promptfoo is not listed as a dependency in package.json and no promptfoo config files were found in the root directory. However, the project has an 'eval' script at line 22 that runs 'tsx --env-file=.env.local scripts/eval-remote-eu-langfuse.ts', suggesting custom evaluation scripts are used instead of promptfoo.
